cards.back: Y = 1/R + j(ωC − 1/ωL). Dual of series Z = R + j(ωL − 1/ωC).
Y = 1/R (min), Z = R (max), LC = open circuit, PF = 1. All source current through R.
R ↔ 1/R, L ↔ C, C ↔ L, V ↔ I, Z ↔ Y. Apply to any series formula to get parallel version.
Q = ωoRC = R/ωoL. Dual of series Q = ωoL/R.
B = 1/RC = ω₀/Q. Dual of series B = R/L.
Parallel LC at resonance: energy oscillates between L and C. Current circulates internally. External impedance = R (max).
IL = IC = Q × Is at resonance. Dual of voltage magnification in series (VL = VC = Q × Vs).

cards.front: Series Z minimum ↔ Parallel Z maximum?
cards.back: Series: Z = R is minimum (max current). Parallel: Z = R is maximum (max voltage). Dual behaviours.

cards.front: Is ω₀ the same for series and parallel?
cards.back: Yes! ω₀ = 1/√LC for both. Only Q and B differ (because they depend on R which enters differently).
Tank circuits in oscillators, bandpass filters, impedance matching, RF tuned amplifiers.
